content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to reach home as soon as possible. He has two options:


Travel with his `BIKE` which takes XXX minutes.
Travel with his `CAR` which takes YYY minutes.
Which of the two options is faster or do they both take same time?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, two integers X,YX, YX,Y representing the time taken to travel with `BIKE` and `CAR` respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, print `CAR` if travelling with Car is faster, `BIKE` if travelling with Bike is faster, `SAME` if they both take the same time.


You may print each character of `CAR`, `BIKE` and `SAME` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `CAR`, `Car`, `cAr` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y, where X is the time (in minutes) taken by the BIKE and Y is the time taken by the CAR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing one of the words: "BIKE" if the bike is faster (X < Y), "CAR" if the car is faster (Y < X), or "SAME" if both take equal time (X == Y).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Bike takes 1 minute, Car takes 5 minutes → BIKE is faster.\nTest case 2: Bike takes 4 minutes, Car takes 2 minutes → CAR is faster.\nTest case 3: Both take 6 minutes → SAME.
First test: Bike 10 > Car 5 → CAR faster.\nSecond test: Bike 5 < Car 10 → BIKE faster.
Both Bike and Car take 7 minutes, so the times are equal.

solution.approach:
For each test case read the two integers X and Y. Compare them:
- If X < Y, the bike is faster → print "BIKE".
- If X > Y, the car is faster → print "CAR".
- Otherwise they are equal → print "SAME".
The algorithm is O(1) per test case, resulting in O(T) total time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
